title: Power Analysis & Precision Planning

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Power analysis determines how many replicates you need to detect effects with desired precision. The precision dashboard tracks your progress toward CI width targets.

sections.heading: Precision Targets
sections.content: The default target is 95% CI width ±0.3 log₂ units. This corresponds to roughly ±30% fold change uncertainty. Adjust targets based on biological significance thresholds.

sections.heading: Factors Affecting Precision
sections.content: Precision improves with more replicates but also depends on:

subsections.heading: Comparison Type
subsections.content: Direct comparisons (same plate) are more precise than indirect comparisons through reference constructs. VIF indicates the precision penalty.

subsections.heading: Session Variance
subsections.content: High session-to-session variance requires more sessions, not just more wells per plate.

subsections.heading: Plate Effects
subsections.content: Including constructs on more plates reduces plate-specific bias.

sections.heading: Recommendations
sections.content: The calculator suggests co-plating strategies to maximize precision gain per experiment. Prioritize constructs with widest current CIs.

why_it_matters: Planning experiments with power analysis prevents wasted resources (too many replicates) or inconclusive results (too few). The precision dashboard provides real-time feedback.
